description: The foundation of all the other factors. Unmuddled attention — the ability to know what is happening while it is happening, without being swept into it.
Investigation of phenomena. The wisdom that turns mindfulness into insight — examining states as they arise, seeing how they come, how they go, and what they are made of.
Energetic effort. Not forceful striving — steady, persistent application. The quality that shows up on the cushion even on the days you don't feel like it.

The joy that arises when the hindrances quiet and the mind collects. Not a goal to chase — a sign that the work is going right. Sometimes physical (tingling, lightness), sometimes a quality of gladness.
emergentWhen: samadhi is steady and the gate holds

description: The settling that follows rapture. Body and mind become tranquil — the heat of viriya matures into cool stability. Pīti was energetic; passaddhi is still.
emergentWhen: samadhi is steady and pīti has arisen

description: Unified collection of mind. The gathering that allows phenomena to be seen clearly without the mind being swept away by them. Samādhi is not a trance — it is the opposite of a scattered mind.

The balance that is not indifference. A mind that is moved by nothing and closed to nothing. The mature quality of a practitioner who has done the work — the last of the seven factors because it rests on all the others.
emergentWhen: all the earlier factors are established
